title: bupleurum tenuissimum subsp. columnae
intro: Bupleurum tenuissimum subsp. columnae, also known as Chinese thunder of heaven, is a plant species within the family Apiaceae, characterized by its small white flowers and slender stems. This plant has been used in traditional Chinese medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiviral compounds, which contribute to its health benefits such as reducing fever, relieving pain, and improving immune function. In herbal preparations, Bupleurum tenuissimum subsp. columnae is often combined with other herbs and used in the form of teas, infusions, or decoctions, as well as in capsule or tablet form. The plant's health benefits are attributed to its ability to regulate the body's energy, known as qi, and promote overall well-being, making it a popular ingredient in traditional Chinese medicine.
